channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Airbus, Air France found guilty of corporate manslaughter over 2009 Rio-Paris crash
item.description: Airbus and Air France were found guilty on ‌Thursday of corporate manslaughter by a Paris appeals court over the 2009 Rio-Paris plane crash that killed 228 passengers and crew, three years after being acquitted in a lower court. Relatives of some of those who died when the Airbus A330 vanishe Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: UK health minister preparing to resign: Times
item.description: Britain's health minister Wes Streeting is preparing to resign and could quit as early as Thursday, the Times reported on Wednesday, adding that he is likely to mount a formal challenge for the party leadership. Streeting had a brief meeting with Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Wednesday ahead of t Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Sweden warns of potential jet fuel shortage
item.description: Sweden is issuing an early warning of a potential shortage of jet fuel stemming from the war in the Middle East, Energy Minister Ebba Busch said on Tuesday. "This is based on the Energy Agency's assessment," Busch told a press conference. Sweden could in a worst case scenario face rationing of av Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: EU mutual assistance clause is unambiguous: Macron
item.description: The EU's mutual assistance clause is unambiguous, French President Emmanuel Macron said on Saturday, after the bloc's leaders asked officials to prepare a blueprint for how it would work amid doubts over Washington's commitment to NATO. President Donald Trump's criticism of NATO for failing to bac Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: BBC will cut up to 2,000 jobs to reduce costs by about 10pc
item.description: The BBC said Wednesday that it plans to cut up to 2,000 jobs to save 10% of its annual budget - 500 million pounds ($677 million) - over the next two years. The layoffs announced during a call with staff are the biggest in more than a decade at the U.K. national broadcaster. "I know this creates Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskiy arrives in Jordan for next leg of Gulf tour
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy arrived in Jordan ​on Sunday as part of a ‌Gulf tour aimed at shoring up defence ties with countries in the region amid ​rising tensions over the Iran ​war. Zelenskiy is seeking support from Gulf ⁠states for Ukraine's war against Russia ​as Western mili Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: France detains two more suspects over foiled Paris Bank of America attack
item.description: French authorities have arrested two more suspects over a ​foiled attack on Bank of America’s Paris offices, the country’s anti-terrorism prosecutor’s office said ‌on Sunday. “Two further individuals were taken into police custody last night as part of the investigatio Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: London Jewish community ambulances set ablaze in antisemitic attack, PM says
item.description: Four ambulances belonging ​to a Jewish community organisation in north London were set ablaze overnight in what British Prime ‌Minister Keir Starmer called "a deeply shocking antisemitic arson attack". The London Fire Brigade, which sent six fire engines and 40 firefighters to the scene, said Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Russia says Ukraine controls 15–17 per cent of Donetsk region
item.description: The Russian-installed head of ​Ukraine's Donetsk region, ‌Denis Pushilin, told Russian president Vladimir Putin ​at a ​meeting shown on state ⁠TV on Tuesday ​that Ukraine controls around ​15-17 per cent of the region. Russia claims the whole of ​the Donetsk region as part ​of Russia and says Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Greece sees deal to buy Italian-made frigates by April, minister says
item.description: Greece hopes to wrap up talks and sign a deal ​for the purchase of Bergamini class ‌frigates from Italy by April, Defence Minister Nikos Dendias said in an interview ​released on Sunday. High-ranking officials from ​Greece and Italy last year signed memorandums for ⁠naval cooperation, opening Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Slovakia PM Fico to meet EU's von der Leyen to push for restart of Druzhba oil flows
item.description: Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico said on Sunday he ‌would meet with EU chief Ursula von der Leyen on Tuesday, as Slovakia and Hungary ramp up pressure on Ukraine to restart Russian oil flows, warning Slovakia could block a major EU ​loan to Kyiv. Russian oil flows through the Druzhba pipelin Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Explosion hits US embassy in Oslo, causing minor damage, police say
item.description: The US embassy in Oslo was hit by an explosion early on Sunday, causing minor damage but no injuries, Norwegian police said. A loud blast was heard near the embassy compound in western Oslo at around 1am (0000 GMT), and smoke was seen rising from the area, eyewitnesses told local media. "We've de Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Myanmar president to lead delegation on official visit to India
item.description: President of the Republic of the Union of Myanmar, Min Aung Hlaing, will pay an official visit to India from May 30 to June 3 at the invitation of Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi. He will be accompanied by a high-level delegation comprising several Cabinet Ministers, senior officials and Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: UN chief eyes leadership cuts as part of reform push
item.description: The United Nations aims to reduce and abolish senior leadership positions over the next two years as part of its reforms, its Secretary-General said on Thursday, as he seeks to streamline the 80-year-old institution which some consider top-heavy. Antonio Guterres, whose 10 years as UN chief draw t Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Ukraine to buy 20 new Gripen jets, Sweden to donate older jets sooner
item.description: Ukraine will buy 20 new Gripen fighter jets and Sweden will donate 16 of an older model next year, Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky and Sweden's Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson said at a joint press conference on Thursday. The two leaders had signed a letter of intent last year paving the way Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Israel carries out strike on Beirut suburbs, first near capital in weeks
item.description: An Israeli strike hit a building in the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital on Thursday, the first strike ‌to hit near Beirut in weeks amid a ceasefire that has failed to halt fighting between Israeli troops and Hezbollah in south Lebanon. The Israeli military said it had condu Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Suspect killed during shooting near White House
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: New York Times sues Pentagon again over media access restrictions
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Mexico denies CNN report on deadly CIA operations against cartels
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump administration says its war in Iran has been ‘terminated’ before 60-day deadline
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump lifts tariffs on UK whisky to toast departing King Charles
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Prosecution of ex-FBI chief Comey over seashell post is flawed, experts say
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Paramedics help passenger give birth just before Delta flight lands
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump safe after shooting at White House correspondents dinner, suspect in custody
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Empty tankers heading to US for oil, gas: Trump
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Kamala Harris says she might run for president again in 2028
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: North American farmers pinch pennies on farm machinery
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: US experts say American strikes on Iran may amount to war crimes
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Suvendu tells ‘illegal’ Bangladeshis to leave
item.description: West Bengal’s newly elected Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari has issued a stern warning to alleged undocumented Bangladeshi nationals, demanding they exit the Indian state immediately or face action. The Times of India, citing national news agency PTI, reported that the Bharatiya Janata Party Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Everest operators must vet climbers better, says ascent record holder
item.description: Mount Everest expedition operators must focus on the experience and knowledge of climbers to lower risks and minimise deaths on the mountain, a British climber and guide who has made the most ascents to the peak by a non-Sherpa said on Monday. Five people have died on Everest this year and some fa Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: West Bengal plans to detain illegal migrants: reports
item.description: The newly-installed West Bengal government has instructed all regional administrators to immediately build containment facilities across the state to house “undocumented” Bangladeshis, Rohingya. According to the Hindustan Times, the Foreigners’ Branch in the SAARC Visa Section of Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: West Bengal makes Vande Mataram recital compulsory in all madrasas
item.description: The West Bengal government has made the recital of Vande Mataram mandatory in all madrasas across the state during morning assemblies, NDTV reports. The order, issued by the Directorate of Madrasah on Wednesday, will apply to all recognised, aided and unaided institutions under the Minority Affair Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: West Bengal allocates 27km for Bangladesh border fence
item.description: West Bengal’s newly formed government has designated a 27-kilometre stretch of land to the Border Security Force (BSF) for the construction of a fence along the Bangladesh border, Press Trust of India (PTI) reports. Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari disclosed the decision during a meeting held Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: India tightens citizenship rules for Bangladesh
item.description: Applicants seeking Indian citizenship under the Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA), 2019 will have to declare whether they hold a valid or an expired passport from Bangladesh, Afghanistan or Pakistan, according to The Hindu. In a notification following an amendment to Citizenship Rules, 2009, the Ind Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Saudi Arabia launched covert attacks on Iran as regional war widened, sources say
item.description: Saudi Arabia launched numerous, unpublicised strikes on Iran in retaliation for attacks carried out in the kingdom during the Middle East war, two Western officials briefed on the matter and two Iranian officials said. The Saudi attacks, not previously reported, mark the first time that the kingdo Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Thailand to cut visa-free stay to 30 days for tourists from 93 countries
item.description: Thailand will cut the visa-free stay period for tourists from 93 countries from 60 days to 30 days to help prevent criminal activity, the foreign minister said. • Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkaeow said on Tuesday that the Ministry of Foreign Affairs will submit a plan to the Cabinet for Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Twelve Pakistani policemen killed in car bombing
item.description: Twelve policemen were dead after a car bombing at a police post in northwestern Pakistan followed by a fight with militants in the city of Bannu, a senior police officer said on Sunday. Three personnel have been found alive and transferred to hospital, the officer added. Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Death toll rises to 14 in Pakistan suicide attack
item.description: The death toll from a suicide attack on a security post in northwest Pakistan rose to 14 police officers, authorities said early Sunday. A self-proclaimed breakaway group of the Pakistan Taliban has claimed the attack, reports AP. A suicide bomber and several gunmen detonated an explosives-laden Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Indian sailor killed, 4 others injured in ship fire near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: An Indian sailor has been killed and four others injured after a dhow caught fire and sank off the Strait of Hormuz, reports Gulf News, citing the Indian Consulate in Dubai. Eighteen Indian crew members were aboard in the wooden vessel when the fire broke out on Saturday. After the vessel capsize Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Thalapathy Vijay likely to take oath as Tamil Nadu CM on Sunday
item.description: India’s actor-turned-politician Thalapathy Vijay met Tamil Nadu Governor RV Arlekar on Saturday evening and staked a claim to form the government in the state of the country. Citing sources, NDTV said his oath-taking is set to take place on Sunday. This high-stakes meeting comes as Vijay's Delivered by PolitePaul service
